page: Amazon Web Services c5d.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by two Intel Xeon 8275CL processors, delivering a total of 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) with a base clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture features dedicated CPU allocation, hyper-threading, and advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI. It is equipped with 192 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

This server includes 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. In benchmark testing, the c5d.metal demonstrates strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a high stress-ng multi-core CPU score and excellent memory bandwidth, reaching 236.4 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. It also excels in high-throughput database operations, achieving 83.23 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and showing strong performance in Passmark database operations and static web serving.

While multi-threaded execution, software compilation, and ray tracing benchmarks are strong, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average to weak, with medium model text generation limited to 5.16 tokens per second. The bare-metal configuration eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly cost-efficient for resource-dense workloads that require direct hardware access. It is best suited for high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8275CL processors running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 192 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. The server delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, alongside a baseline network speed of 25 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate exceptional throughput for database operations, Redis SET tasks, and software compilation, though single-core performance and LLM text generation are relatively average. Lacking a GPU, it is not designed for heavy AI model training. Instead, the c5d.metal offers a highly cost-efficient, resource-dense profile for bare-metal workloads, making it an excellent fit for high-performance computing, large-scale database hosting, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.metal is a bare-metal compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, built for high-throughput database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized platform featuring ninety-six vCPUs and high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) Intel Xeon 8275CL processor at 3.6 GHz
192 GB DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong multi-core CPU, database operations, and Redis SET performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with dedicated bare-metal access
Best suited for high-performance computing, databases, and software compilation
